content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcar- PRACTTcEs C oiraurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
Apri|20,2006
Larry Minor
Agri-Empire
P.O. Box 490
San Jacinto, CA 92581
RE: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-06-052
Dear Mr. Minor:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Since your request seeks
general guidance, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.2
QUESTION
Does the Act prohibit you from holding two public offices concurrently?
CONCLUSION
There is nothing in the Act that prohibits a local public official, such as you, from
holding two public offices concurrently. However, other laws outside the jurisdiction of
the Fair Political Practices Commission ("Commission") may restrict a public official
from holding two public offices simultaneously, if those offices are determined to be
"incompatible" offi ces.
FACTS
You currently hold a position as a Board Member of the Valley Wide Recreation
and Park District. You are contemplating running for a position as a Board Member of
the Lake Hemet Municipal Water District. You ask whether the Act prohibits you from
holding two public offices concurrently.
I Governme.tt Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Infbrmal assistance does not pro,rid" the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 83 I 14; regulation 18329(c)(3).)
rqa..ii


File No. I-06-052
Page No. 2
ANALYSIS
There is nothing in the Act that prohibits a local public official, such as you, from
holding two public positions concurrently. Holding a position as a Board Member of the
Valley Wide Recreation and Park District while also holding a position as a Board
Member of the Lake Hemet Municipal Water District does not, in itself create a potential
disqualifiring conflict of interest under the Act. (See e.g. Johnson Advice Letter,No. I-
04-244; Hernandez Advice Letter, No. I-04-240; and Foster Advice Letter, No. A-02-
t2s.)
However, other laws outside the jurisdiction of the Commission may restrict a
public official from holding two public offices simultaneously, if those offices are
determined to be "incompatible" offices. You are urged to check with the legal counsel
retained by your special district to determine if there are other laws that may be
applicable, in light of the facts you present, that may cause a problem under the doctrine
of incompatible offices. The Commission's advice is limited to matters arising under the
Act. (Regulation 18329(bX8Xd).)
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By:
Brian G. Lau
Counsel, Legal Division
BGL:rd
